items.title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
items.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

items.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
items.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

items.title: Realta Fusion generates electricity directly from a fusion reaction, an apparent first

items.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
items.description: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. items.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
items.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork.  The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins that leave little room for error. Research shows AI-enabled predictive models can improve crop…

items.title: Repositioning retail for the AI era
items.description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping retail, but not in the ways consumers might immediately notice. items.title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
items.description: AI is booming. New use cases are emerging each day. To capitalize on the technology’s potential, enterprises require data at scale.
